qtype: Time duration calculation, total cost calculation
tex:
Ray owns an electrical shop.
\newline

(a) The table shows the opening times of the shop.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Sunday & Closed \\
\hline
Monday & Closed \\
\hline
Tuesday & 08$\,$00 to 12$\,$30 and 13$\,$30 to 17$\,$00 \\
\hline
Wednesday & 08$\,$00 to 12$\,$30 and 13$\,$30 to 17$\,$00 \\
\hline
Thursday & 08$\,$00 to 12$\,$30 and 13$\,$30 to 17$\,$00 \\
\hline
Friday & 08$\,$00 to 12$\,$30 and 13$\,$30 to 17$\,$00 \\
\hline
Saturday & 08$\,$00 to 13$\,$00 and 14$\,$00 to 19$\,$00 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Work out how many hours the shop is open in one week.
\newline

(b) Saeed buys 2 ovens costing \$440 each, 4 grills costing \$184 each and 3 fridges costing \$1280 each.
\newline

Calculate the total cost.
\newline

(c) Alice buys 3 batteries costing \$2.85 each.
\newline

Work out how much change she receives from \$10.
\newline

(d) Cherie works 32 hours one week and she is paid \$8.48 per hour.
\newline
\\
In another week she works 37 hours.
\newline
\\
For each hour over 32 hours she works, she is paid 1.25 times her hourly rate.
\newline

Calculate her pay for the week she works 37 hours.
\newline

(e) Ray buys a toaster for \$36.
\newline
\\
When he sells it he makes a profit of 40\%.
\newline

Calculate the selling price of this toaster.
\newline

\hline

\hline

\hline
Saturday & 08\,00 to 13\,00 and 14\,00 to 19\,00 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Work out how many hours the shop is open in one week.
\newline

Saeed buys 2 ovens costing \$440 each, 4 grills costing \$184 each and 3 fridges costing \$1280 each.
\newline

Calculate the total cost.
\newline

Alice buys 3 batteries costing \$2.85 each.
\newline

Work out how much change she receives from \$10.
\newline

Cherie works 32 hours one week and she is paid \$8.48 per hour.
\newline
\\
In another week she works 37 hours.
\newline
\\
For each hour over 32 hours she works, she is paid 1.25 times her hourly rate.
\newline

Calculate her pay for the week she works 37 hours.
\newline

\\
When he sells it he makes a profit of 40\%.
\newline

Calculate the selling price of this toaster.

_rawBody:
Ray owns an electrical shop.

\begin{parts}
\item The table shows the opening times of the shop.

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Sunday & Closed \\
\hline
Monday & Closed \\
\hline
Tuesday & 08\,00 to 12\,30 and 13\,30 to 17\,00 \\
\hline
Wednesday & 08\,00 to 12\,30 and 13\,30 to 17\,00 \\
\hline
Thursday & 08\,00 to 12\,30 and 13\,30 to 17\,00 \\
\hline
Friday & 08\,00 to 12\,30 and 13\,30 to 17\,00 \\
\hline
Saturday & 08\,00 to 13\,00 and 14\,00 to 19\,00 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Work out how many hours the shop is open in one week.

\AnswerLine[][hours]
\Marks{3}

\item Saeed buys 2 ovens costing \$440 each, 4 grills costing \$184 each and 3 fridges costing \$1280 each.

Calculate the total cost.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item Alice buys 3 batteries costing \$2.85 each.

Work out how much change she receives from \$10.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\item Cherie works 32 hours one week and she is paid \$8.48 per hour.\\
In another week she works 37 hours.\\
For each hour over 32 hours she works, she is paid 1.25 times her hourly rate.

Calculate her pay for the week she works 37 hours.

\WorkingSpace{5cm}

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{4}

\item Ray buys a toaster for \$36.\\
When he sells it he makes a profit of 40\%.

Calculate the selling price of this toaster.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\end{parts}

tex:
Mei and Jian each make a four-faced dice as shown in the diagram.
\newline
\\
The faces on each dice are numbered 1, 2, 3 and 4.
\newline

(a) Mei throws her dice 90 times and records the scores.
\newline
\\
The pie chart shows the results.
\newline

(i)  Write down the mode.
\newline

 Work out how many times she scores 1.
\newline

(b) Jian throws his dice 90 times and records the scores.
\newline
\\
The pie chart shows the results.
\newline

Write down the median.
\newline

(c) Write down two different comparisons between the results for Mei and the results for Jian.
\newline

1.
\newline
 

2.
\newline

content.content:
Write down the mode.
\newline

Work out how many times she scores 1.
\newline

tex:
(a) The diagram shows a scale drawing of Joel's rectangular garden.
\newline
\\
The scale is 1 centimetre represents 8 metres.
\newline

Scale: $1\,\text{cm}$ to $8\,\text{m}$
Find the actual area of his garden.
\newline

(b) The diagram shows a rectangular gate, $FGHJ$, in Joel's garden.
\newline

$GJ = 2.1\,\text{m}$ and $FG = 0.85\,\text{m}$.
\newline

Find $FJ$.
\newline

(c) 
The diagram shows Brenda's rectangular garden.
\newline
\\
There is a patio in the shape of a quarter-circle.
\newline
\\
She wants to grow grass in the shaded part of the garden.
\newline
\\
She needs $40\,\text{g}$ of grass seed per square metre.
\newline
\\
Grass seed is sold in $1\,\text{kg}$ bags which cost \$6.80 per bag.
\newline

Calculate the cost of the grass seed she needs to buy.
\newline

qtype: algebraic manipulation, substitution, factorization, changing the subject, solving linear equations
tex:

\newline

\[3a - 5b + 2a + b\]

(b) $P = 3x^2 - xy$

Find the value of $y$ when $P = 90$ and $x = 5$.
\newline

(c) Factorise completely.
\newline

(i)  $6x - 18$

 $25x^2 + 10x$

(d) $T = 8d - 3$

Make $d$ the subject of this formula.
\newline

(e) Solve these equations.
\newline

(i)  $\dfrac{x}{6} = 12$

 $7x - 4 = 3x + 2$

qtype: Ratio sharing, compound interest, reverse percentage, limits of accuracy (money context)
tex:
Rita and Henry own an investment business.
\newline

(a) They share the profit in the ratio Rita : Henry $= 3 : 5$.
\newline
\\
In one year they make a profit of \$$2\,400\,000$.
\newline

Calculate Rita's share of the profit.
\newline

(b) Henry invests \$$160\,000$ at a rate of 2.5\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of this investment at the end of 3 years.
\newline

(c) Rita invests \$$12\,000$ at a rate of $r$\% per year.
\newline
\\
The value of her investment at the end of one year is \$$12\,408$.
\newline

Work out the value of $r$.
\newline

(d) Rita and Henry decorate their office.
\newline
\\
The cost, \$$c$, is \$$10\,800$, correct to the nearest hundred dollars.
\newline

Complete this statement about the value of $c$.
\newline

\[ \leqslant c < \]

content.content:
They share the profit in the ratio Rita : Henry $= 3 : 5$.
\newline
\\
In one year they make a profit of \$$2\,400\,000$.
\newline

Calculate Rita's share of the profit.
\newline

Henry invests \$$160\,000$ at a rate of 2.5\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of this investment at the end of 3 years.
\newline

Rita invests \$$12\,000$ at a rate of $r$\% per year.
\newline
\\
The value of her investment at the end of one year is \$$12\,408$.
\newline

Work out the value of $r$.
\newline

Rita and Henry decorate their office.
\newline
\\
The cost, \$$c$, is \$$10\,800$, correct to the nearest hundred dollars.
\newline

Complete this statement about the value of $c$.
\newline

\[\makebox[2.5cm]{\dotfill} \leqslant c < \makebox[2.5cm]{\dotfill}\]

stem.content:
Rita and Henry own an investment business.
\newline

qtype: completing table of values and drawing graph of quadratic function
tex:
The table shows some values for $y = x^2 + x - 5$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-4$ & $-3$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & $0$ & $1$ & $2$ & $3$ \\
\hline
$y$ & $7$ & & $-3$ & $-5$ & $-5$ & & & $7$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) Complete the table.
\newline

(b) Draw the graph of $y = x^2 + x - 5$ for $-4 \leqslant x \leqslant 3$.
\newline

(c) Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of this graph.
\newline

(d) Use the graph to solve the equation $x^2 + x - 5 = 0$.
\newline

$x = $    or   $x = $

content.content:
Complete the table.
\newline

Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of this graph.
\newline

stem.content:
The table shows some values for $y = x^2 + x - 5$.
\newline
